We have the capability to process the coronal sections of the cerebral hemispheres, when needed.
The cost of regular-size (1X3) slides is $ 42 each for manual preparation and $ 45 each, as prepared by IHC stainer .

column14: Yes. Most blocking is performed by Dr. Matthew Frosch and the neuropathology fellows and residents in the MGH Neuropathology department.
column19: One hemisphere is formalin fixed, the contralateral side is dissected fresh and flash-frozen. Frozen tissue is stored long term at -80 C and fixed tissue is currently stored in formalin. FFPE blocks and slides used for neuropathological diagnosis are stored in the ADRC offices for use and reference. Average cost of slide is variable and depends on stain; manual or autostainer, antibody in question, silver nitrate, etc.

column14: No; Core Leader or other neuropathologist at center (N=3)
column19:
Left hemibrain (typically unless asymmetric atrophy/disorder) formalin fixed and processed. A single coronal slab from the right hemibrain (typically) also fixed and processed at the level of the hippocampus to capture bilateral hippocampi, middle frontal gyri, and superior temporal gyri.

IHC with HIER in diH20, citrate or Tris-EDTA on commercial food steamer for 30 minutes (formic acid pretreatment for select stains). IHC reagents include standard hydrogen peroxide, Sigma goat serum (G9023), primary antibody (variable), anti-mouse or rabbit IgG secondary antibody (R&D Systems VisUCyte HRP) and DAB Substrate Kit (BD Biosciences) totaling roughly $5-10 per slide (variably due to cost of different primary antibodies used). Estimate does not include personnel time or equipment usage/service.
